title: Agreement controller and override candidate
notes: Twenty-ninth-round mutation generated after agreement cards repeatedly split controller choice, licensed override, and attraction/error pressure. This graph is a narrow agreement module built from source-backed cards, not a general grammar account and not eligible for projective credit until held-out agreement cards are added.

edges.rationale: Unit construal, distributivity, or participant-identity readings provide contextual warrant for identifying the agreement controller.
Agreement feature alignment depends on first identifying the relevant controller rather than simply choosing the nearest noun phrase.
Licensed override patterns can satisfy agreement alignment even when they depart from simple head-feature matching.
For agreement cards, controller-feature alignment is a necessary component of licensing in the target repertoire.
Lower licensing of an agreement value predicts greater repair or reformulation pressure.
Licensed agreement values tend to have higher production probability in relevant opportunity contexts, without making raw frequency identical to licensing.
Community licensing provides warrant for agreement-related grammaticality attribution without being identical to the attribution.
Repair pressure is contextual evidence that an agreement value is being treated as problematic.
A salient non-controller feature source can increase processing or retrieval cost in agreement production and judgment.
A salient attractor can increase production of agreement errors in some tasks while having little effect in others.
Processing or retrieval difficulty can lower reported acceptability independently of agreement licensing.
Acceptability reports provide contextual evidence for attribution but can be perturbed by processing or task framing.
Speeded, correctness-framed, naturalness-framed, and editing tasks can shift reported acceptability for agreement contrasts.
Task framing can cue whether respondents treat an agreement contrast as grammar, style, error, or acceptable override.
Standard-language ideology can raise explicit condemnation of agreement variants independently of production probability.
Condemnation can influence attribution while remaining separable from controller alignment and licensing.

score_status.scope: Agreement controller identification, agreement feature alignment, licensed override patterns, notional agreement basis, and retrieval-attractor salience; not a general grammaticality account.
